Customers are still sparse, and it doesn’t seem to be bustling. I wonder if they’re making enough money to not go under?
Thanks to coming here almost every day lately, I've completely gotten used to the scenery.
Partly due to the lack of customers,
she’s become like my personal bartender. I feel bad for the other bartender.
As usual, I can’t tell if he’s in a good mood or a bad one. He seems friendly yet somehow distant, which is tricky.
I’ve never seen him shake a cocktail, so I’m not sure if he’s a top-tier bartender. At least he’s good at cooking.
We know each other well. Even without saying anything, certain things come out. It's nice to have someone familiar around; it makes things easier.
Phew.
I know this isn't something I should say to a customer, but,
seeing your face so frequently is starting to get a bit boring, huh?
Ah.
Well, that's true for both of us.
Oh, I see.
I said that jokingly, but you were being serious just now, weren't you?
Huh?
Um, that’s not good.
Well, it's not like it bothers me.
Hmph.
By the way, what happened with Jennifer the other day?
W-what do you mean what happened?
W-what's going on?
Your anxious attitude makes it so obvious something happened. Wish you happiness.
Are you hungry? You look like you haven’t had a proper lunch.
This morning when I went to the office, it had been ransacked.
Huh?
That sounds rough.
Yeah.
But, nothing was stolen. Weird, right?
Um.
Well, it could mean there was truly nothing taken, or maybe they didn’t find what they were looking for.
It's probably one of those.
Things have been getting noisy around you lately, huh?
I wonder why?
Maybe because your social circle has widened?
As long as I'm alive, I can deal with anything else.
Phew.
It's become quite the hassle.
Isn't it always a hassle for you lately?
That's true, but this time it's even more difficult.
What on earth happened?
You should be careful; in the end, you might even lose your life.
Has Jennifer come since then?
No, not at all. Since she achieved her goal, isn't it fine now?
What the heck is that?
Well,
it all started when I came here to meet.
I mean, it's rude to say this, but for such a beauty to
come to meet is a bit strange, isn't it?
W-what is it?
That's really rude!
If I offended you, I'm sorry, but she is
What did Jennifer say?
No, let's not. We don't want to lose our regular customers.
I haven't seen anything at this shop.
Regarding Eva's body, it seems she was dismembered using a scalpel.
A scalpel!?
Yes. I heard that, so I thought I should report it.
The perpetrator of the serial murder case also dismembers with a scalpel, right?
It really does sound like it’s shaping up to fit that scenario. At this point, the possibility that the perpetrator of the serial murder case is involved is pretty high.
Eva might have been the person closest to uncovering the truth behind the serial murder case.
I was curious about what he said, so I poked around Garrison.
So, how did it go?
I hope the results never come again!
It was an attack.
It’s definitely certain that the baseball team is involved.
Is that so?
If we take our time, they might slip up on their own.
Still, what kind of scheme could there be? It’s intriguing.
Apparently, only Meryl went to see her daughter.
What about your daughter and Yuriko?
You think that's strange, right? That she didn't go to Yuriko.
Yuriko, are you sure you haven't mistaken the meeting place?
That must have been tough for Meryl.
I'm being completely treated like the bad guy.
Well, I suppose that's true.
I hope Meryl's mood improves.
Hey, have you seen Meryl?
I can't believe Meryl knows this place.
Uh.
Hmm, where could Meryl be?
It's somewhere that's not here.
Did you say today was a mother-daughter meeting?
No, she's not here. I even checked the promised place.
What on earth happened?
Maybe they're enjoying themselves at some shop.
No way Cindy came here.
No. Why would she come here?
Right?
I was hoping she might come to apologize to me since she blew off her meeting with her mom.
If that's the case, it would be natural for her to visit the office. Maybe she'll just show up tomorrow.
I hope so.
Is there something bothering you?
Cindy was looking forward to meeting her mom, and she's not the kind of girl to break promises.
It's probably just a typical whim of a teenage girl. I don't think you need to overthink it.
I hope so.
If you're that worried, why not consult the police or the FBI? Though they probably won't take it seriously.
There's no shortage of missing persons in New York. Plus, it hasn't been confirmed that she's actually missing.
There's no point in worrying about it right now.
Is it okay for you to be here?
I cleaned up properly, and anyway, now Meryl is facing her mother and Yuriko isn’t here.
But isn't it worrying about the office?
That’s not it, it’s not like the key was broken, it’s just been ransacked. Is it some kind of warning?
Who would do that, and for what purpose?
If I knew that, it wouldn't be a struggle.
I have no idea.
It's a strange story.
Don't you have any clues?
It seems like they don’t think well of Jennifer. It’s true that she can be easily misunderstood.
No way.
That guy isn’t interested in me, is he? He said he wasn’t gay.
I don’t know the details of his past, but it’s clear he’s familiar with the underground.
It's wise not to ignore his advice.
Talking to him strangely puts my mind at ease. He should quit being a bartender and become a therapist.
Was there something behind Jennifer and me having that kind of incident? If so, then why with someone like me?
I wonder what they're doing now. Can’t walk around carelessly, probably just wasting time in some bland hotel room?
If possible, I want to meet them right now.
I wonder if they wouldn’t mind if I went.
I feel sorry for Eva. If I could, I want to find the culprit and take revenge.
To think the method used to kill Eva was the same as in the serial murders. So it was definitely the same culprit.
Speaking of which, didn’t Eva say she found out who the serial killer was? Is that why she got killed?
That time Garrison was so flustered, it was definitely something serious. I wonder if Guy is being held by Nets.
The director is really something; even though he's my boyfriend, he tells me to bring him here.
I just can't face Meryl. After what happened, there's no way she'd listen to me.
Where could he have gone? I never imagined he'd break our promise.
I wonder if he suddenly couldn't forgive his mother for abandoning him? I thought I’d finally gotten rid of one burden, but I’m back to square one.
I'm worried about the office. But that doesn't mean I can just hang around there.
As I said, I truly don't understand the intentions of the office vandal.
Was it just a light vandalism meant as a warning?
If I go back and find it’s been vandalized again...
Cleaning up will be a nightmare.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
